title: SCHC uses compress/decompress while other codecs use encode/decode or from_bytes/write_to
description:
Naming conventions across codec crates:

lichen-schc/src/codec.rs:
  pub fn compress(packet, out) -> Result<usize, SchcError>
  pub fn decompress(data, out) -> Result<usize, SchcError>

lichen-senml/src/cbor.rs:
  pub fn encode(records, out) -> Result<usize, CborError>
  pub fn decode(data, buf) -> Result<usize, CborError>

lichen-coap, lichen-link, lichen-rpl all use:
  Type::from_bytes(data) -> Result<Self, Error>
  type.write_to(out) -> Result<usize, Error>

The SCHC naming is intentional (matches RFC 8724 terminology) but differs from the codebase convention.

What goes wrong: Minor discoverability issue. Users searching for 'encode' or 'decode' functions won't find SCHC's compression. Not critical since the RFC terminology is correct, but worth documenting the rationale in the module docstring.
